first_paragraph: The **Golden Aspen Rally**, formerly **Aspencade**, is an annual [motorcycle rally](/wiki/Motorcycle_rally "Motorcycle rally"). Along with [Americade](/wiki/Americade "Americade") in New York and the [Lone Star Rally](/w/index.php?title=Lone_Star_Rally&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lone Star Rally (page does not exist)") in Texas, it is one of the [American Motorcyclist Association's](/wiki/American_Motorcyclist_Association "American Motorcyclist Association") three annual rallies,[[1]](#cite_note-1) drawing an estimated 30,000 attendees.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is held annually on the third week of September in [Ruidoso, New Mexico](/wiki/Ruidoso,_New_Mexico "Ruidoso, New Mexico") and was ongoing from 1969 to 2019, and will resume in 2021.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Motorcycle Rally

| Golden Aspen Rally | |
| --- | --- |
| Locations | [Ruidoso](/wiki/Ruidoso,_New_Mexico "Ruidoso, New Mexico"), [New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico"), USA |
| Founded | 1969 |
| Attendance | 30,000 |
| Website | [motorcyclerally.com](http://motorcyclerally.com) |

The **Golden Aspen Rally**, formerly **Aspencade**, is an annual [motorcycle rally](/wiki/Motorcycle_rally "Motorcycle rally"). Along with [Americade](/wiki/Americade "Americade") in New York and the [Lone Star Rally](/w/index.php?title=Lone_Star_Rally&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lone Star Rally (page does not exist)") in Texas, it is one of the [American Motorcyclist Association's](/wiki/American_Motorcyclist_Association "American Motorcyclist Association") three annual rallies,[[1]](#cite_note-1) drawing an estimated 30,000 attendees.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is held annually on the third week of September in [Ruidoso, New Mexico](/wiki/Ruidoso,_New_Mexico "Ruidoso, New Mexico") and was ongoing from 1969 to 2019, and will resume in 2021.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") caused 2020's rally to be cancelled.
